description:
## Background

Beadsmith currently derives the Current Workspace from the desktop process working directory. That development shortcut makes launch location an ambient, process-global input, prevents dependable workspace switching, and lets an in-flight load risk associating one repository data with another.

This epic delivers the completed workspace-management specification as an implementation roadmap. A Workspace is the Git repository root initialized for Beadwork. The backend owns the persisted Workspace Catalog, Pending Workspace, and optional Current Workspace; frontend issue-loading calls never supply a path. A Workspace becomes Current only after validation, all Issue Explorer views load, and persistence succeeds.

## What to build

Deliver a compact sidebar Current Workspace selector that lets a user choose, remember, restore, remove, and atomically switch local Beadwork Workspaces. The app must keep the old Current Workspace and its Issue Explorer snapshot visible until a new candidate is completely loaded and durably committed.

## Technical Approach

The child beads deliberately sequence an expand-and-contract foundation, initial selection, catalog lifecycle, atomic switching, and final real-desktop proof. Every product slice includes its own tests; the final bead consolidates cross-slice evidence and removes obsolete cwd-launch documentation.

## Considerations

- Use the native directory picker and validate selected paths through Beadwork, then store the validated Git root rather than a selected subdirectory.
- Persist a 100-entry MRU Workspace Catalog in a backend-owned Tauri store.
- Automatic issue refresh, custom Workspace names, and special symlink alias deduplication are out of scope.
- Existing process-cwd and `--workspace` test support must disappear by the end of this epic; it is not an alternate product mode.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A user can select and use multiple local Beadwork Workspaces without relaunching Beadsmith.
- [ ] Current Workspace never changes before a complete candidate load and durable store commit succeed.
- [ ] Known, unavailable, pending, and Current Workspace behavior is covered by automated tests and real desktop acceptance.

## Test Plan

- Run Rust unit and integration tests plus frontend unit and integration tests in every child slice.
- Run the real desktop WebDriver suite after the final migration.
- Run `pnpm check` and generated TauRPC binding checks before closing the epic.
title: Implement Workspace management
